priorities: legacy and succession planning for grandchildren
redLines: no exposure to high‑beta speculative asset classes
avoid speculative US software/technology and AI stocks
no aggressive strategy shifts or volatile growth equities
do not allocate to mega‑cap tech positions
preferredSectors: consumer staples (e.g., Nestlé, Procter & Gamble, Walmart, Costco)
industrial manufacturers and hardware (e.g., Siemens, ASML)
healthcare and pharma (e.g., Johnson & Johnson, Novartis)
high‑grade sovereign and G7 government bonds

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: long‑term, multi‑generational wealth preservation for grandchildren
investmentBehavior.liquidity: low immediate liquidity needs; occasional cash draws for personal projects (e.g., home renovation) while keeping core equity intact

lifeEvents.description: CHF 150,000 withdrawn from cash accounts for a structural renovation of the holiday home in Flims.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Liquidity was taken from cash reserves; core equity positions were left untouched.

lifeEvents.description: Succession planning discussion; client requested a strict flag to block any high‑beta speculative asset classes.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforced the defensive stance and formalized a non‑negotiable barrier against speculative investments.

lifeEvents.description: Opened a minor savings account for the oldest grandson, emphasizing traditional saving virtues.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Introduced inter‑generational savings focus; likely future allocation to low‑risk, cash‑like instruments.

lifeEvents.description: Account handover protocol initiated due to RM Thomas Keller’s early retirement.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Ensured continuity of the existing defensive allocation under a new relationship manager.

lifeEvents.description: Final farewell lunch; client expressed trust in the bank’s institutional heritage to protect the conservative core portfolio.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: No change; reaffirmed confidence in current strategy.

lifeEvents.description: Introductory call with new RM Sarah Meier; client reiterated expectation of a quiet, global defensive value approach.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Confirmed continuation of the existing conservative mandate.

lifeEvents.description: Requested that upcoming June bond coupons be swept to the family foundation.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Directs fixed‑income cash flow to philanthropic use without altering asset allocation.

keyQuotes: "I want to sleep at night, not speculate on Silicon Valley cloud bubbles."
"These abstract software and AI firms are valued on pure hype. It feels like the dot‑com crash of 2000 all over again."
"We expect the same quiet, global defensive value approach that Thomas Keller provided. No aggressive strategy shifts."
